mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger immediate risk-off positioning, depressing S&P 500 returns over the next trading week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of triggered instances, and the t-statistic for return difference versus baseline remains below 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, which sustains corporate earnings momentum and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in both series fails to produce positive 60-day returns in > 60% of cases, and the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the index of industrial production crosses above its 12-month moving average, it confirms a supply-side recovery that alleviates capacity constraints and supports equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in > 50% of crossing events, and the rolling Sharpe ratio of the signal remains below 0.5 over out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising core inflation combined with a stable or rising federal funds rate forces markets to price in tighter financial conditions, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: Positive CPIAUCSL growth alongside non-falling FEDFUNDS yields positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances, and the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate disposable income and consumer spending capacity, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show no positive correlation with payroll growth (Rank IC < 0.01), and earnings revisions consistently diverge from payroll trends.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in housing starts exceeding 5% signals weakening residential investment and broader demand fragility, prompting equity deleveraging and negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of triggered instances, and housing weakness fails to transmit to equity risk premia in regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence where industrial production rises while real GDP growth decelerates indicates supply-side efficiency gains that compress input costs and boost near-term equity returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO growth alongside negative GDPC1 growth yields negative 5-day returns in > 50% of cases, and the interaction term fails to improve out-of-sample R-squared.
